graphPolicy.retrofitTruthRule: Observed KEP text, source paths, and test paths are evidence only; they are not maintainer approval.
graphPolicy.implementationRule: This first Kubernetes pass is read-only. A later target change requires a narrower selected record, pinned checkout, allowed files, forbidden flows, and explicit user approval.

nodes.intentClaim: KEP-753 defines sidecar containers as restartable init containers that run through the Pod lifecycle without blocking Pod termination.
The KEP goals include first-class sidecar pattern support, Job completion improvement, initContainer/sidecar startup choreography, injection support, and longer-running sidecars.
The KEP explicitly excludes arbitrary container dependency graphs, sidecar security enforcement, sidecar-specific privilege separation, and alpha termination ordering guarantees.
keps/sig-node/753-sidecar-containers/README.md is the formal intent and rollout source for this retrofit mapping.
pkg/api/v1/pod/util.go contains restartable init-container helpers such as IsRestartableInitContainer.
pkg/kubelet/kuberuntime/kuberuntime_container.go owns kubelet init-container action computation that keeps restartable init containers running.
pkg/api/v1/resource/helpers.go is connected to KEP resource request accounting concerns and should be checked before resource-related sidecar changes.
pkg/api/v1/pod/util_test.go and pkg/kubelet/kuberuntime/kuberuntime_container_test.go are unit-test surfaces for restartable init and kubelet lifecycle behavior.
test/e2e_node/container_lifecycle_test.go contains node e2e coverage for sidecar/restartable init-container lifecycle scenarios.
This pass must not mutate kubernetes/kubernetes, kubernetes/enhancements, or claim upstream approval.
Future sidecar work must not broaden into arbitrary dependency graphs, security enforcement, privilege separation, or unapproved termination-ordering semantics.
This read-only fixture cannot claim Kubernetes build, unit, integration, conformance, or e2e validation.
A read-only PBE retrofit mapping can recover KEP-753 intent and connect it to Kubernetes code/test surfaces before selecting any target change.

edgeIntent.claim: The KEP README is the formal source for the sidecar containers product intent in this retrofit mapping.
The selected feature scope is lifecycle support for restartable sidecar-style init containers, not a generic container orchestration language.
The KEP non-goals prevent future work from treating sidecar support as dependency graphs, security enforcement, privilege separation, or unapproved termination ordering.
Pod utility restartable-init classification is a core code surface for recognizing the sidecar mechanism.
Kubelet runtime lifecycle action computation is the central implementation surface for starting and keeping restartable init containers alive.
The KEP resource accounting concerns require resource helper surfaces to be checked before any resource-related sidecar change.
The KEP test-plan obligation maps to pod utility and kubelet runtime unit-test surfaces for restartable init behavior.
The KEP lifecycle and restart scenarios map to node e2e container lifecycle coverage.
The first large-project dogfood should only recover and map intent before selecting any Kubernetes target change.
The read-only KEP mapping must not mutate external repositories, open upstream PRs, or claim maintainer approval.
The fixture records candidate verification surfaces but cannot claim Kubernetes test results.
